name: Mom's Apple Cobbler
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Melt butter in a 13x9x2-inch glass or metal baking dish.
3. Heat sugar and water in a medium saucepan until the sugar dissolves; set aside.
4. Cut shortening into flour using a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
5. Add milk to the dough and stir with a fork only until the dough leaves the sides of the bowl.
6. Turn dough out onto lightly floured board or pastry cloth and knead gently just until smooth.
7. Roll out to a 12x10-inch rectangle.
8. Stir together apples and cinnamon in a mixing bowl.
9. Sprinkle apples evenly over the dough.
10. Roll up jelly-roll style, beginning with a long side.
11. Press edges to seal.
12. Cut into 16 slices, each about 1/2 inch thick.
13. Arrange slices in the pan with melted butter.
14. Pour sugar syrup carefully around and over rolls.
15. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes or until golden brown.
16. Cool for 15 minutes before serving.
17. Serve warm with cream or ice cream, if desired.
